headline: GRAINS DRIFT LOWER AHEAD OF WASDE
subheadline: Cattle bounce continues as screwworm confirmation removes uncertainty.
lead: Corn closed $4.17, down another half-cent as the grain complex extends its five-day slide into this morning's WASDE release. The funds are stepping back ahead of the supply numbers, open interest down across the board, but the selling isn't selling pressure - it's positioning. Cattle held the bounce at $241.78, up 0.9%, as screwworm confirmation paradoxically steadied the complex by removing the uncertainty premium.
the_takeaway: Wait for WASDE; the funds are positioning, not panicking.
teaser: Grains drift lower ahead of WASDE as cattle bounce holds on screwworm clarity.

one_number.unit: consecutive days of soybean losses before today's rebound
one_number.context: Soybeans snapped their longest losing streak since March, finishing higher despite the broader grain weakness. The streak broke on decent flash sales reported by USDA, but the rally feels thin with funds still rotating out. Eight days of losses tells you the carry trade is working against old crop, not that demand disappeared.

yesterdays_call.summary: Called cattle bounce real but narrow, with feeder weakness keeping breakdown alive.
yesterdays_call.note: Feeders stayed flat while live cattle gained, exactly the selective recovery pattern expected.

sections.title: GRAINS DRIFT AHEAD OF WASDE
sections.body: Corn lost another half-cent to $4.17 as the complex extends its slide into this morning's **WASDE release**. The selling isn't selling pressure, it's positioning - funds stepping back ahead of supply revisions, open interest dropping across the board. Soybeans broke an eight-day losing streak to close $11.20, down just 3 cents, on decent flash sales reported by USDA. Wheat held better at $5.85, off a penny, as the defensive rotation keeps it steadiest in the complex. The 11 AM numbers reset the story.
sections.bottom_line: Funds positioning for WASDE, not selling conviction.

sections.title: CATTLE BOUNCE HOLDS ON CLARITY
sections.body: Live cattle gained $2.17 to $241.78 as the **screwworm confirmation** paradoxically steadied futures by removing uncertainty. The pest is confirmed, the management protocols are known, and the discount got priced Tuesday. Feeders barely budged at $354.52, up just 35 cents, keeping the complex split that signals selective recovery. Processing capacity is the real constraint, not disease fear. The bounce is real but narrow, exactly what you'd expect when the unknown becomes known.
sections.catalyst: New World screwworm confirmation removes uncertainty premium
sections.bottom_line: Confirmation removed uncertainty, processing capacity still the constraint.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: bounce held, feeders lagging confirms selective recovery

sections.title: ENERGY BREAKS ON HORMUZ REVERSAL
sections.body: Crude oil tumbled $2.54 to $89.22, down 2.8%, as Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure declaration this morning **reversed into reports of continued tanker traffic**. The oil market is learning not to chase the headlines - Kuwait and other producers are moving cargoes in dark mode, Japan secured July supply volumes matching last year. Natural gas fell 2.4% to $3.12 on storage builds outpacing seasonal draws. The Hormuz premium is deflating as fast as it built.
sections.catalyst: Iran Hormuz closure declaration contradicted by continued tanker traffic reports
sections.bottom_line: Market learning not to chase Hormuz headlines.

outside_the_pit.title: Trump questions USMCA renewal ahead of 2026 review
outside_the_pit.body: President said Wednesday the US might not renew the trade agreement with Mexico and Canada. Farmers for Free Trade expressed alarm, but the 2026 review was always scheduled. Markets aren't pricing it yet.

outside_the_pit.title: Dairy checkoff lawsuit challenges fund use
outside_the_pit.body: Three Wisconsin farmers sued over dairy checkoff funds going to private third-party organizations. The suit targets mandatory funding mechanisms that have been standard practice. Class III milk weakness isn't helping the optics.

outside_the_pit.title: India secures crude supply through August
outside_the_pit.body: Indian refiners boosting purchases from UAE, Africa, and Brazil to offset Hormuz concerns. Another sign the oil market is routing around the strait rather than pricing permanent disruption. Geographic diversification working.

spread_to_watch.label: December corn / July corn carry
spread_to_watch.commentary: The carry's holding while funds step back, which tells you storage is still working. If WASDE cuts ending stocks meaningfully, this spread flattens first. Watch for the carry to break under 25 cents - that's when old crop gets scarce enough to matter.

basis.headline: Eastern Corn Belt firming on ethanol restart
basis.body: **Basis is talking** in Ohio and Indiana as ethanol plants come back online after maintenance. Producers with old-crop corn in storage have a window the futures board isn't pricing. Western Corn Belt staying soft, consistent with the seasonal flow. The spread between eastern and western basis is **14 cents and widening**.

weekly_thread.question: Will the grain complex break out of its five-day slide this week?
weekly_thread.status_text: The slide continues into WASDE day, but soybeans snapped their losing streak. Friday's response to today's supply numbers decides if this was positioning or the start of something bigger.

the_more_you_know.title: Why WASDE day always thins the crowd
the_more_you_know.body: Corn's open interest dropped across the board ahead of this morning's WASDE, and there's a reason: the report can move markets 3% in either direction based on a single line revision. Smart money steps back rather than guess which way the number breaks. The volume comes back after the release when the direction is clear. Today's positioning phase typically sees 15-20% lower volume than the session average, but tomorrow's follow-through gets the real conviction.

watch_list.desc: WASDE release; corn ending stocks above 2.1B bu keeps pressure on, below 1.9B flips the narrative
CBOT settlement; how grains close after WASDE sets tomorrow's opening tone
Weekly export sales; soybeans need 400K+ MT to confirm the flash sales momentum

daily_quote.text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

source_summary: CME Group, USDA reports, Reuters energy surveys, agricultural trade publications
